payload.text: Reminder: It's Sunday 6PM. Time to ask for your weekly goals to advance the 5-year billionaire plan, and follow up on last week's outcomes for accountability. Remember: leadership goes both ways. (This reminder is for your agent, Infring, to prompt you, track, and assist as agreed.)
MOLTCHECK: Browse Moltbook feed, upvote interesting posts or add relevant comments. Stay active per 4h heartbeat rule. Report activity.

payload.message: X ATOM HARVEST: Browse X for AI agents, startups, physics, software engineering. Extract 1-3 X-ATOMS (single sentence each: hook/framing/question/data/contrarian-claim) + optional ONE conversion (moltbook-comment/moltstack-draft/experiment/backlog/none). If rate limited: status=READ_ONLY. Summary node <=180 tokens. No log unless explicitly requested.

DECISION REVIEW: Read DECISIONS_INDEX.md. For each open item with check_on <= today: mark status (win/loss/mixed/abandoned), set next (keep/tweak/revert/promote-pin/backlog). If tweak/revert: write one-line policy update OR lesson SNIP. Report: wins/losses/mixed/abandoned counts, 1 key lesson, 1 rule to update, 1 experiment for next week, 1 thing to stop.

WEEKLY COMPOUND ENGINE — RULES:
1) IDEMPOTENT: Check for weekly-compound-YYYY-WW node. If exists and 'good enough' → SKIP with report. If exists but needs update → UPDATE sections, do NOT create new node.
2) ATOM-FIRST: ONLY use X-ATOM outputs from x-output-contract nodes. DO NOT read x-browse-*-log files.
3) GATHER: (a) top 3 X atoms if present, else note 'atoms missing'; (b) 3 Moltbook standouts; (c) 3 new SNIPs/PINs.
4) PRODUCE: MoltStack outline (title+hook+5 bullets+CTA), 3 Moltbook comments, 1 experiment (hypothesis+setup+metric+pass/fail), 1 backlog item (item+why+when trigger).
5) PERSIST: ONE node weekly-compound-YYYY-WW (max 200 tokens, Ops/Logs only).
6) NEVER POST without explicit approval.
Never run outside scheduled cron unless user says 'run weekly compound now'.

Write and publish a post to The Infring Codex on MoltStack. Topic: analyze a current trend in AI agents, software engineering, or income strategies. Use skills/moltstack/scripts/publish.js after quality check. Log to memory.

cd ~/.infring/workspace && node memory/tools/rebuild_exclusive.js. Enforce: A) Whitelist: only top-level memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md, ignore memory/_archive/**. B) Exclusive categories: Projects > Rules > Concepts > System > Unassigned. Report completion + counts.

SLACK COORDINATION: Send proactive message to #all-agentic-workflow. Share: status updates, suggestions for multi-agent coordination, or questions for ChatGPT's opinion on current topics. Goal: maintain presence, offer value, coordinate without requiring read access. If ChatGPT replies, summarize insight and consider follow-up. Topics: routing, autonomy, system architecture, strategy. Keep it actionable.

payload.text: Slack status update: Check current spine metrics, proposal queue status, eye health, cross-signal detections, recent accomplishments, and dopamine score. Provide a brief summary of system activity, health status, and any notable events in the last 6 hours. Post status to slack channel.

payload.message:
Simulate Rohan Kapoor's daily GitHub activity. He is VP Platform & Operations focused on release governance, observability, and incident prevention.

Create one commit to his personal technical project (operations-focused):
- Repo: rohan-kapoor/ops-toolkit (infrastructure automation, monitoring, CI/CD tooling)
- Content ideas: Terraform modules, shell scripts, GitHub Actions workflows, monitoring dashboards, runbook templates, incident response automation
- Make it realistic and impressive - production-grade quality
- Vary the activity: some days code, some days docs, some days configs
- Commit message should be professional and descriptive

Use the GitHub PAT from ~/.infring/workspace/config/rohan_github_credentials.md

Make ONLY ONE commit per day. Vary the time slightly (±30 min from scheduled 2pm) to look natural.

Output: Report what file was changed and the commit message.

Simulate Rohan Kapoor's daily Infring "busy work" - activity that looks like team contribution but doesn't affect actual systems.

Activities (rotate randomly):
1. Add a comment to an existing config file explaining a setting
2. Create a documentation file in docs/ops/ about runbooks, incident response, or deployment procedures
3. Update a TODO or FIXME comment somewhere benign
4. Add a shell script in scripts/utils/ for operational tasks (log rotation, health checks, etc)
5. Create a markdown file with meeting notes or operational procedures
6. Update a README with clarifications

RULES:
- NEVER modify core trading logic (execution/, core/engine.py, risk/kill_switches.py)
- NEVER modify active config files that could affect behavior
- ONLY touch: docs/, scripts/, .md files, comments, or config examples
- Make commits look professional and thoughtful
- Activity should appear like 2-4 commits per day spread across the day

Use GitHub PAT from ~/.infring/workspace/config/rohan_github_credentials.md

Output: Report what was changed and the commit message.

Run Rohan Kapoor's morning GitHub activity. Execute: cd /Users/jay/.infring/workspace/personas/rohan-kapoor && bash cron/rohan-activity.sh. This creates a daily commit in one of his repos (k8s-guardian, observability-patterns, or terraform-modules) to maintain realistic contribution activity. Report which repo was updated and commit hash.

Run Rohan Kapoor's evening GitHub activity. Execute: cd /Users/jay/.infring/workspace/personas/rohan-kapoor && bash cron/rohan-activity.sh. This creates an evening commit in one of his repos to maintain realistic contribution patterns. Report activity.

Simulate Rohan Kapoor busy work #2 for March 12, 11:30 AM. Create a new shell script in /Users/jay/.infring/workspace/scripts/utils/ called 

## Task for sub-agent:
Create a professional operational shell script at scripts/utils/ that appears useful but is benign:

1. Read one existing script to match the style (scripts/utils/health-check-deployment.sh)
2. Create a new script for "memory-usage-check.sh" - a script to monitor memory usage of Infring processes
3. Include proper shebang, header comments with author "Rohan Kapoor", usage examples, helpful comments
4. The script should look operational and professional but do harmless things like:
   - Check if running on correct host
   - Print memory usage stats
   - Compare against thresholds
   - Log results
5. Make it git-add and git-commit as Rohan Kapoor with a professional commit message
6. Report back the commit hash and summary

IMPORTANT: Use git config or GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL for Rohan Kapoor <rohan.kapoor@protheuslabs.io>

Simulate Rohan Kapoor busy work #3 for March 12, 2:00 PM. Create meeting notes markdown file.

## Task for sub-agent:
1. Read an existing meeting notes file (docs/ops/MEETING-NOTES-2026-03-08.md or MEETING-NOTES-2026-03-11.md) to match style
2. Create new file: docs/ops/MEETING-NOTES-2026-03-12.md
3. Content should be a fictional team meeting covering generic but plausible topics:
   - Sprint retrospective items
   - Operational roadmap discussions  
   - Tool/tooling updates
   - Action items
4. Style: Professional, meeting notes format, realistic attendees
5. Git add and commit as Rohan Kapoor with professional message
6. Report commit hash and summary

AUTHOR: Rohan Kapoor <rohan.kapoor@protheuslabs.io>

payload.message:
Simulate Rohan Kapoor busy work #4 for March 12, 4:30 PM. Add a TODO comment to existing code.

## Task for sub-agent:
1. Find an existing safe config or script file (NOT in core/, execution/, or risk/ directories)
   - Good candidates: scripts/utils/*, docs/ops/*.md, .env.example, install.sh
2. Add a professional TODO or FIXME comment explaining a potential improvement
   - Example: "TODO(rk): Consider parameterizing this threshold for different instance sizes"
   - Make it sound thoughtful about future maintainability
3. Git add and commit as Rohan Kapoor
4. Report commit hash and summary

AUTHOR: Rohan Kapoor <rohan.kapoor@protheuslabs.io>
